items.content: MONTREAL, QC / ACCESS Newswire / July 9, 2026 / First Canadian Graphite Inc. (TSXV:FCI) (OTCQB:GRAPF) ("FC graphite" or the "Company") is pleased to announce that the successful virtual annual and special general meeting of shareholders was held on July 7, 2026. A total of 16,292,900 common shares (approximately 36.23% of the outstanding common shares) were represented in person or proxy. 1. NUMBER OF DIRECTORS Resolution to set the number of directors of the Company for the ensuing year at five (5) persons was passed by 97.39% FOR, 2.61% AGAINST and 0.00% Withheld/Abstained. 2. ELECTION OF DIRECTORS Each of the following individuals were elected as directors of the Company as approved by a vote by ballot, for a term expiring at the conclusion of the next annual meeting of shareholders of the Company or until their successors are elected or appointed, as follows: Name Votes "For" (%) Votes "Against" (%) Votes "Withheld/Abstained" (%) John LaGourgue 96.25% 0.00% 3.75% Michael Iverson 96.22% 0.00% 3.78% Rich Beaven 99.97% 0.00% 0.03% Hon. Christian Paradis 99.97% 0.00% 0.03% Alberto De Luca 99.97% 0.00% 0.03% The Company is pleased to announce the appointment of the new board members and would like to introduce them along with the current directors to the shareholders. Below is a brief biography for each. John LaGourgue, Chief Executive Officer, President and Director - Mr. LaGourgue brings extensive experience in leadership, capital markets, commercial and governmental relations, business development, and operations in the resource sector. He has a proven track record in project oversight, corporate finance, risk management, and commercial operations. This includes over 25 years in senior executive roles, board positions, advisory roles, and investments in private and public companies with a focus on microcap issuers. Mr. LaGourgue has successfully led commercial and mining exploration programs, secured more than $100 million in debt and equity funding, and advanced projects through multiple stages. From 2016 to late 2024 Mr. LaGourgue was a senior officer and/or director of Vicinity Motor Corp. Until his appointment to CEO of First Canadian Graphite he was managing his personal investment portfolio and was active in the MicroCap community. Michael Iveson, Director (independent) - President of Triple K Ventures Ltd., a private investment and consulting company; director and executive officer of numerous public companies since January 1998. Rich Beaven (independent) - Mr. Rich Beaven is an equity research analyst and portfolio manager since 1995 with a primary focus on the materials, energy and durables sectors. Mr. Beaven started with a $2b wealth management firm in Spokane, WA and subsequently became a founding member and minority owner of Signia Capital in 2002. Transitioned to co-ownership of Signia Capital in 2020. Hon. Christian Paradis, P.C., LL.B., Director (independent) - Hon. Christian Paradis was called to the Quebec Bar in 1997, elected Member of Parliament from 2006 to 2015. Appointed to cabinet in 2008 and notably served as Minister of Industry and Natural Resources. Previously practiced law, specializing in corporate law. In 2016 served as Senior Vice President of Strategic Development at the multinational security firm GardaWorld. Currently a senior advisor and CEO of Paradis Solutions & Associates, a firm founded in 2020 which focuses on business development. Member of the King's Privy Council for Canada and recipient of the Queen Elizabeth II Diamond Jubilee Medal (2012) and the King Charles III's Coronation Medal (2025). Alberto De Luca, Director (independent) - Mr. Albert De Luca, a Fellow CPA is a retired tax partner of Deloitte Canada. Albert spent the greater part of his professional career working with leading organizations in Canada and abroad which invest extensively in innovation which enhances their industrial base, from ITC to metals & mining and many others. Such experience was key to helping attract some of the largest foreign investments to Canada. Albert's point of view has consistently been sought out by the federal and the Quebec governments in those policy matters which are relevant for industries which form the backbone of Canada and Quebec's economy. Mr. De Luca chaired and is board member of numerous Quebec government sponsored organizations which promote industrial innovation (such as Axelys, CENTECH and ADRIQ). 3. APPOINTMENT OF AUDITOR The appointment of Smythe LLP, Chartered Professional Accountants, as the auditor of the Company, to hold office until the next annual meeting of shareholders of the Company, and the authorization of the directors of the Company to fix the auditor's remuneration, was approved by a resolution passed by a vote by ballot, with 16,266,313 (99.84%) total votes cast "FOR" and 26,586 (0.16%) Withheld/Abstain. 4. APPROVAL 2026 OMNIBUS SHARE INCENTIVE PLAN Resolution to approve the 2026 Omnibus Share Incentive Plan passed by a resolution of disinterested shareholders by a vote by ballot, with 11,383,501 (94.04%) total votes cast "FOR" and 721,621 (5.96%) "AGAINST". OTHER MATTERS The board of directors held a meeting to appoint the following: Appointment of Officers John LaGourgue Chief Executive Officer and President Scott Kelly Chief Financial Officer Antoine Fournier Vice-President Exploration Shelley McDonald Corporate Secretary Appointment of Audit Committee John LaGourgue Michael Iverson Alberto De Luca Compensation, Corporate Governance and Nominating Committee Charter The board adopted and approved a Compensation, Corporate Governance and Nominating Committee Charter and the following directors were be appointed to the Committee for the ensuing year: John LaGourgue Rich Beaven Hon. Christian Paradis About FC graphite FC graphite is engaged in the exploration of critical minerals and is committed to advancing its Lac Guéret South high-grade graphite property to support applications in energy transition and advanced technologies. The Lac Guéret South Project, formerly known as the Berkwood Project, borders Nouveau Monde Graphite's Uatnan graphite project in the southwest Manicouagan reservoir area, 234 km north-northwest of Baie-Comeau, in the Côte-Nord administrative region of Québec on the Nitassinan (ancestral territory) of the Pessamit Innu. Lac Guéret South features surface-exposed graphite and a historical NI 43-101 compliant resource at Zone 1 containing: 1.76 million tonnes indicated at 17% Cg and 1.53 million tonnes inferred at 16.4% Cg (NI 43-101 Technical Report Mineral Resource Estimate). The property also features several other zones with surface graphite samples, offering strong exploration potential. In early 2026, the Company increased its land holdings to 167 km², establishing one of the most extensive claim portfolios within the Lac Guéret Graphite District. With substantial exploration opportunities remaining and total investments exceeding $10 million, FCI is actively engaged in expanding its resources and advancing toward the completion of its Preliminary Economic Assessment (PEA). The Company possesses sufficient funding to further advance this project throughout 2026. Pessamit Innu First Nation Engagement FC graphite formally acknowledges that the Lac Guéret South Project is situated within the Nitassinan (ancestral territory) of the Pessamit Innu First Nation. The Company remains dedicated to cultivating respectful, transparent, and cooperative relationships with local Indigenous communities at every stage of the project's development. On Behalf of the Board of Directors First Canadian Graphite Inc. Signed: John LaGourgue, CEO & Director For more information, contact: info@FCGraphite.com (604) 838-3376 https://fcgraphite.com/ Neither TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this News Release.

items.title: Onza de plata extiende pérdidas por debajo del nivel de 60 dólares
items.summary: El precio de la onza de plata se replegó el miércoles por tercera jornada en el mercado de futuros de Nueva York, ampliando su caída por debajo de nivel simbólico de 60 dólares.   La caída del precio del metal plateado está asociada al repunte de los precios del petróleo... The post Onza de plata extiende pérdidas por debajo del nivel de 60 dólares appeared first on Mining México .
items.content: El precio de la onza de plata se replegó el miércoles por tercera jornada en el mercado de futuros de Nueva York, ampliando su caída por debajo de nivel simbólico de 60 dólares. La caída del precio del metal plateado está asociada al repunte de los precios del petróleo y la la preocupación por la inflación, ⁠después de que el presidente de Estados Unidos, Donald Trump, afirmara que el acuerdo de paz provisional con Irán había «terminado». Lo anterior, luego de que el Mando Central de EEUU reportara ataques contra buques comerciales en el estrecho de Ormuz, a lo que Washington respondió con bombardeos aéreos. En represalia, la Guardia Revolucionaria de Irán confirmó haber atacado instalaciones militares estadounidenses en Baréin y Kuwait. En la actualidad, los metales preciosos han perdido su etiqueta de refugios de inversión, debido a las expectativas de altas tasas de interés. Los operadores apuestan a que los bancos centrales tendrán que mantener altos los costos de endeudamiento para frenar el impacto inflacionario por los altos precios de las energía y los combustibles. Desde que comenzó la guerra a finales de febrero, el precio de la plata ha caído más de 36%, presionado por los altos precios del petróleo y combustibles, lo que que ha elevado las expectativas de tipos de interés más altos para combatir la inflación, un escenario que perjudica al oro. Te puede interesar: Onza de plata se repliega 21% en el trimestre; En el mercado Commodity Exchange (Comex), el contrato más activo de la plata perdió 3.1% para cerrar en 58.60 dólares la onza. Al cierre del segundo trimestre, la onza de plata sufrió una caída de 21%, su peor revés en un periodo similar desde el primer trimestre de 2020. El 29 de enero, la onza del metal plateado alcanzó un máximo histórico de 121.64 dólares. Desde ese récord, la onza de plata pierde 52%. En lo que va de año, la onza de plata experimenta una caída de 23%. Sin embargo, en los últimos 12 meses, aún se anota una ganancia de 60%, pero está lejos del rally histórico del año pasado. En 2025, la plata se disparó 150% y se anotó su mejor rendimiento anual desde 1979. El rally histórico del año pasado estuvo asociado a la escasez de la oferta industrial, que hiló su quinto año consecutivo de déficit global. Para más información del sector minero y energético también puedes consultar el portal de noticias en tiempo real de Industry News Mx . The post Onza de plata extiende pérdidas por debajo del nivel de 60 dólares appeared first on Mining México .

items.title: Onza de oro profundiza caída por aumento de las tensiones en Medio Oriente
items.summary: El precio de la onza de oro se replegó el martes por segunda jornada en el mercado de futuros de Nueva York, cerca del piso simbólico de 4,000 dólares.   La caída de los precios del metal dorado está asociada al repunte de los precios del petróleo y la la... The post Onza de oro profundiza caída por aumento de las tensiones en Medio Oriente appeared first on Mining México .
items.content: El precio de la onza de oro se replegó el martes por segunda jornada en el mercado de futuros de Nueva York, cerca del piso simbólico de 4,000 dólares. La caída de los precios del metal dorado está asociada al repunte de los precios del petróleo y la la preocupación por la inflación, luego de que Donald Trump declarara el fin del acuerdo con Irán. El acuerdo de entendimiento, que había sido negociado por Pakistán el mes pasado para otorgar una tregua de 60 días a las negociaciones, colapsó definitivamente tras una serie de ofensivas militares. Las tensiones escalaron luego de que el Mando Central de EEUU reportara ataques contra buques comerciales en el estrecho de Ormuz, a lo que Washington respondió con bombardeos aéreos. En represalia, la Guardia Revolucionaria de Irán confirmó haber atacado instalaciones militares estadounidenses en Baréin y Kuwait. El aumento de los precios de la energía puede impulsar la inflación y, potencialmente, llevar a los bancos centrales a subir los tipos de interés para contener las presiones inflacionarias. Si bien el oro suele considerarse una protección contra la inflación, este metal, que no genera rendimientos, tiende a perder atractivo en un entorno de tipos de interés elevados. En la actualidad, los metales preciosos han perdido su etiqueta de refugios de inversión, debido a la fortaleza del dólar y s expectativas de altas tasas de interés. Desde que comenzó la guerra a finales de febrero, el precio del oro ha caído más de 20%, debido al aumento de los precios de la energía. Te puede interesar: Onza de oro cierra en baja por regreso de tensiones geopolíticas En el Commodity Exchange (Comex), el contrato más activo del oro perdió 1.8% para cerrar en 4,088.70 dólares la onza . La onza del oro vienes de una caída mensual de 11%. El miércoles 24 de junio, en las operaciones intradía, la onza descendió hasta 3,988.00 dólares, su menor valor desde el 6 de noviembre de 2025. Adicionalmente, la onza del metal precioso registró su primer descenso trimestral desde 2024 y al más pronunciado desde el segundo trimestre de 2013, al registrar una contración de 14% entre abril y junio. Como resultado de la reciente volatilidad, el metal dorado baja 6.5% en lo que va del año. Antes del conflicto bélico, acumulaba una ganancia de 20% en los primeros dos meses del año. Ahora, la onza de oro experimenta una caída 22% desde que comenzó la guerra en Medio Oriente a finales de febrero (5,230 dólares). A pesar del actual ajuste, la onza del oro aún mantiene una ganancia de 23% en los últimos 12 meses. Expectativas eclipsadas Bank of America recortó un 14% su pronóstico promedio del oro para 2026 a 4,360 dólares ante una Reserva Federal restrictiva, aunque prevé alcanzar los 5,000 dólares al concluir el ajuste monetario. La semana pasada, Analistas de ING recortaron sus previsiones del oro para finales de 2026: ahora proyectan un promedio de 4,300 dólares la onza en el tercer trimestre y 4,600 en el cuarto, reduciendo sus estimaciones previas. Cabe recordar que el pasado 2 de marzo, el oro cruzó por primera vez en su historia el nivel de los 5,400 dólares en operaciones electrónicas, superando el récord intradía de 5,362.40 dólares registrado el 29 de enero. Para más información del sector minero y energético también puedes consultar el portal de Industry News Mx. The post Onza de oro profundiza caída por aumento de las tensiones en Medio Oriente appeared first on Mining México .

items.title: Crypto Market Update: 'Regulation Crypto' Agenda Slated for July
items.summary: Here's a quick recap of the crypto landscape for Wednesday (July 8) as of 10:00 p.m. UTC. Get the latest insights on Bitcoin, Ether and altcoins, along with a round-up of key cryptocurrency market news. Bitcoin price update Altcoin price update Today's crypto news to know ​Bitcoin price update Bitcoin (BTC) was priced at US$62,149.60, down by 2.4 percent over the past 24 hours. Utkarsh Ahuja at Moon Pursuit Capital advises investors to distinguish between a temporary price relief rally and a gen
items.content: Here's a quick recap of the crypto landscape for Wednesday (July 8) as of 10:00 p.m. UTC. Get the latest insights on Bitcoin, Ether and altcoins, along with a round-up of key cryptocurrency market news. Bitcoin price update Altcoin price update Today's crypto news to know ​Bitcoin price update Bitcoin (BTC) was priced at US$62,149.60, down by 2.4 percent over the past 24 hours. Utkarsh Ahuja at Moon Pursuit Capital advises investors to distinguish between a temporary price relief rally and a genuine cycle recovery. “In my view, the recent rally in BTC was encouraging, but today’s pullback reinforces that it’s still too early to call the start of a new bull cycle. “While ETF inflows have improved, broader market participation and trading volumes remain relatively subdued, particularly during the seasonally quieter summer months. That leaves BTC highly sensitive to macro and geopolitical developments, including renewed tensions involving Iran. “I believe investors should focus less on short-term price moves and more on whether liquidity is meaningfully expanding. Until participation broadens and volumes strengthen, I view rallies as constructive, but ultimately as relief rallies rather than confirmation of a durable trend reversal.” Ether (ETH) was priced at US$1,736.63, trading 2.9 percent lower over the last 24 hours. Altcoin price update XRP (XRP) was priced at US$1.09, trading 2.9 percent lower over the past 24 hours. Solana (SOL) was trading at US$77.28, trading 5.2 percent lower over the past 24 hours. ​Today's crypto news to know Bitget Wallet crosses 100 million users Bitget Wallet says it has crossed 100 million users globally, with daily payment users now outnumbering traders for the first time in the platform’s history. The data points to crypto wallets becoming a practical part of everyday finance, especially in emerging markets, with H1 2026 card spending reaching US$31 million. Spending in Southeast Asia, South Asia, Africa, and Latin America was up 416 percent, with active cardholders averaging 9.4 transactions per month. Tether invests in Brazil's Mercado Bitcoin Tether is deepening its push into crypto’s financial infrastructure with a US$20 million investment in Mercado Bitcoin, Brazil’s largest crypto exchange. The strategic round backs Mercado Bitcoin’s expansion into tokenization, payments, credit and capital markets across Latin America, while also underscoring Tether’s broader strategy of taking equity stakes in the rails that distribute its stablecoin rather than limiting itself to issuance alone. “Tether’s mission is to build open, accessible, and efficient financial infrastructure for the world,” said Paolo Ardoino, CEO of Tether. “Mercado Bitcoin has built exactly that, a regulated, full-stack on-chain financial platform serving millions of users across one of the world’s most dynamic financial markets. Its depth of regulatory licensing, tokenization infrastructure, and integrated financial services is unmatched in Latin America. We look forward to supporting Mercado Bitcoin’s next phase of growth as a strategic partner and investor.” SEC set for major crypto overhaul The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is gearing up for a massive regulatory overhaul this July with the release of its highly anticipated "Regulation Crypto" agenda. The proposal aims to clarify the rules around digital assets and establish clear guidelines for the industry while protecting investors. Central to the package are new registration exemptions that would allow crypto startups to bypass the full securities registration process for up to four years as they develop their networks. During this temporary exemption period, projects would still be legally required to provide necessary financial statements and disclosures to their investors. Additionally, the SEC is considering a fundraising exemption, championed by Chairman Paul Atkins , that would let startups raise up to US$75 million in any 12-month period without undergoing full agency registration. The overhaul also proposes a crucial investment contract safe harbor designed specifically for decentralized finance (DeFi) projects and token issuers. Under this safe harbor, once developers are no longer the driving managerial force behind a project and the network becomes fully decentralized, the token would no longer be treated as a security. Reserve Bank of India reiterates call for crypto ban India's cryptocurrency policy remains firmly in limbo, but internal government documents reveal that key authorities continue to push for tighter restrictions and a potential blanket ban, according to a Reuters report . The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has doubled down on its long-standing hardline stance by officially backing a policy that leans toward the complete prohibition of digital assets. According to recent documents from May and June, the central bank argues that domestic banks and financial institutions should be entirely barred from holding, trading, or having any exposure to cryptocurrencies or privately issued stablecoins. The RBI believes isolating the traditional financial system from crypto is necessary to prevent market volatility from spreading to the wider economy. In tandem with the central bank's warnings, India’s Income Tax Department has raised its own alarm regarding the severe difficulties of monitoring trades executed on overseas crypto exchanges. Tax officials stress that this lack of visibility makes it incredibly difficult to enforce compliance, significantly increasing the risk of widespread tax evasion among domestic traders. Despite these coordinated warnings from major agencies, the Indian government has not yet taken a definitive legislative stand on whether to regulate or outright ban virtual digital assets. Gemini leads crypto IPO losses with 89 percent collapse from debut The post-IPO landscape for cryptocurrency companies show major digital asset listing since mid-2025 currently trading well below its debut-day price. Leading the massive downward trend is the Gemini exchange, which has seen its stock plunge a staggering 89 percent from its US$37 opening trade last September down to just US$4.19. Across the sector, BitGo is sitting 77 percent below its January 2026 debut of US$22.43 and Bullish shares sinking roughly 71 percent from their US$90 open. Other recent public entrants have also stumbled: eToro trades are down 42 percent from its opening print, while Figure and Circle have dropped 14 and 6 percent, respectively. The persistently weak market performance has completely frozen the upcoming pipeline for future crypto listings, forcing several major firms to push back their planned 2026 debuts. Kraken's parent company Payward paused its listing earlier this spring, while industry heavyweights like Grayscale, Consensys, and Ledger have similarly postponed their IPO preparations until market conditions eventually stabilize. Don't forget to follow us @INN_Technology for real-time news updates! Securities Disclosure: I, Meagen Seatter, hold no direct investment interest in any company mentioned in this article. Securities Disclosure: I, Giann Liguid, hold no direct investment interest in any company mentioned in this article. The opinions expressed in these interviews do not reflect the opinions of the Investing News Network and do not constitute investment advice. All readers are encouraged to perform their own due diligence.

items.title: Top 10 Biggest Gold Mines in the World and How They Operate
items.summary: Introduction to the World’s Largest Gold Mines Gold has been one of the most sought-after precious metals throughout human history. Today, massive mining operations across the globe extract thousands of […]
items.content: Introduction to the World’s Largest Gold Mines Gold has been one of the most sought-after precious metals throughout human history. Today, massive mining operations across the globe extract thousands of tonnes of gold every year to meet global demand. From open-pit excavations to underground tunnels stretching kilometers deep, the world’s biggest gold mines are remarkable feats of engineering, technology, and human determination. In this post, we explore the top 10 biggest gold mines in the world , where they are located, how much gold they produce, and how they operate. 1. Muruntau Gold Mine – Uzbekistan The Muruntau Gold Mine in Uzbekistan is widely recognized as the largest open-pit gold mine in the world . Located in the Kyzylkum Desert, this massive operation spans over 3.5 kilometers in length and nearly 3 kilometers in width, with a depth reaching over 600 meters. How Muruntau Operates The mine is operated by Navoi Mining and Metallurgical Combinat (NMMC), a state-owned company. It uses a combination of open-pit mining, heap leaching, and carbon-in-pulp (CIP) processing to extract and refine gold. The mine produces approximately 2 million ounces of gold per year , making it a dominant force in global gold production. Giant haul trucks, drill rigs, and sophisticated chemical processing plants are all part of the daily operations at Muruntau. 2. Carlin Trend – Nevada, USA The Carlin Trend in Nevada is not a single mine but a collection of gold deposits stretched across a 40-mile zone that together make it one of the most productive gold-mining regions on Earth. Nevada accounts for more than 70% of all gold produced in the United States. How Carlin Trend Operates Operated primarily by Nevada Gold Mines , a joint venture between Barrick Gold and Newmont Corporation, the Carlin Trend uses both open-pit and underground mining techniques. The ore is processed using autoclaves and roasters to treat the refractory ore before gold is extracted using cyanide leaching processes. Annual production from this region exceeds 3 million ounces of gold . 3. Grasberg Mine – Papua, Indonesia The Grasberg Mine in the remote highlands of Papua, Indonesia, is one of the world’s largest gold and copper mines. Operated by PT Freeport Indonesia , a subsidiary of Freeport-McMoRan, this mine sits at an elevation of over 4,000 meters above sea level. How Grasberg Operates Grasberg has transitioned from open-pit mining to underground block caving, one of the most cost-effective underground mining methods for large, low-grade orebodies. The mine uses a series of tunnels, conveyor systems, and ore processing facilities to produce gold and copper concentrates. It produces over 1 million ounces of gold annually along with significant amounts of copper. 4. Olympic Dam – South Australia, Australia Located in South Australia, the Olympic Dam mine is a massive polymetallic underground mine operated by BHP . While it is primarily known for uranium and copper production, it is also one of the world’s largest gold producers. How Olympic Dam Operates Olympic Dam uses underground mining methods combined with on-site smelting and refining. The operation is entirely underground, with over 450 kilometers of underground roads and tunnels. Gold is extracted as a by-product of copper smelting and refining. The mine produces approximately 130,000 to 150,000 ounces of gold per year . 5. Pueblo Viejo Mine – Dominican Republic The Pueblo Viejo Mine in the Dominican Republic is one of the most significant gold mines in the Caribbean and Latin America. It is jointly owned by Barrick Gold (60%) and Newmont Corporation (40%) . How Pueblo Viejo Operates The mine uses open-pit mining techniques, and the ore is processed using pressure oxidation (POX) technology to break down the refractory sulfide ore. Gold and silver are then extracted using conventional cyanide leaching. Pueblo Viejo produces approximately 800,000 ounces of gold per year and is considered one of the lowest-cost gold producers globally. 6. Lihir Gold Mine – Papua New Guinea Located on Lihir Island in Papua New Guinea, the Lihir Gold Mine is one of the world’s largest gold deposits. The mine sits inside an ancient volcanic crater and is operated by Newcrest Mining . How Lihir Operates One of the unique challenges of Lihir is the geothermal activity within the ore body, which requires continuous pumping and cooling systems to maintain safe operating conditions. The mine uses open-pit mining combined with pressure oxidation and carbon-in-leach (CIL) processing to extract gold. Annual production is approximately 700,000 to 800,000 ounces of gold . 7. Kibali Gold Mine – Democratic Republic of Congo The Kibali Gold Mine , located in the northeastern Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), is one of Africa’s largest gold mines. It is operated by Barrick Gold in partnership with AngloGold Ashanti and Sokimo, a Congolese state mining company. How Kibali Operates Kibali uses a combination of open-pit and underground mining methods. The mine features three hydropower stations that supply the majority of its power, making it one of the more sustainable large-scale mining operations in Africa. The processing plant uses a combination of gravity concentration and carbon-in-leach techniques. Kibali produces around 750,000 ounces of gold per year . 8. Cortez Mine – Nevada, USA Another Nevada-based operation, the Cortez Mine is one of the largest individual gold mines in North America. It is operated by Nevada Gold Mines , the Barrick-Newmont joint venture. How Cortez Operates The Cortez complex includes both open-pit and underground operations. The Pipeline and Crossroads deposits are mined using open-pit methods, while the deeper Cortez Hills underground mine uses long-hole stoping techniques. Gold extraction uses heap leaching for oxide ores and autoclave processing for refractory sulfide ores. Cortez produces approximately 500,000 to 700,000 ounces of gold annually . 9. Boddington Gold Mine – Western Australia, Australia The Boddington Gold Mine in Western Australia is one of Australia’s largest gold producers and is operated by Newmont Corporation . It is also a significant copper producer. How Boddington Operates Boddington is an open-pit operation that uses truck-and-shovel mining to extract ore from the pit. The processing plant uses a combination of conventional milling, flotation concentration, and CIL leaching to produce gold and copper. Boddington produces approximately 700,000 ounces of gold per year and is known for its efficient and modern operational practices. 10. Goldstrike Mine – Nevada, USA The Goldstrike Mine , also located in Nevada and operated by Nevada Gold Mines , is one of the most historically productive gold mines in the United States. It has been in operation since the 1980s and remains a key asset in the Barrick-Newmont portfolio. How Goldstrike Operates Goldstrike encompasses both open-pit and underground operations. The underground mine uses roaster technology to process refractory ore, which is a defining feature of the operation. Gold is then recovered using conventional cyanide leaching and carbon adsorption. Annual production is approximately 500,000 ounces of gold . Key Mining Techniques Used in Large-Scale Gold Mining Understanding how these massive gold mines operate requires a basic knowledge of the mining methods and processing techniques commonly used across the industry. Open-Pit Mining Open-pit mining is the most common method used in large gold mines. It involves removing large quantities of surface material (overburden) to expose the ore beneath. Giant excavators, blast drilling, and haul trucks are used to move ore from the pit to processing facilities. This method is cost-effective for large, low-grade deposits close to the surface. Underground Mining When ore deposits are too deep for open-pit mining, underground mining methods are used. Techniques include block caving, long-hole stoping, and cut-and-fill mining. Underground operations are more expensive but allow access to high-grade ore bodies at greater depths. Heap Leaching Heap leaching is a process where crushed ore is piled on lined pads and irrigated with a cyanide solution that dissolves the gold. The gold-rich solution is then collected and processed to recover the metal. This method is cost-effective for low-grade oxide ores. Carbon-in-Leach (CIL) and Carbon-in-Pulp (CIP) These processes involve adding activated carbon to a cyanide slurry containing dissolved gold. The gold adsorbs onto the carbon, which is then stripped and processed to produce gold bullion. CIL and CIP are commonly used for higher-grade ores and more complex mineralogy. Pressure Oxidation (POX) Pressure oxidation is used to treat refractory sulfide ores where gold is locked within sulfide minerals. The ore is subjected to high temperature and pressure in the presence of oxygen, which oxidizes the sulfides and makes the gold accessible for cyanide leaching. Environmental and Social Considerations in Large Gold Mines The world’s largest gold mines have a significant environmental and social footprint. Modern mining companies are increasingly focused on sustainable mining practices , including: Water management: Minimizing water usage and preventing contamination of local water sources. Tailings management: Safely storing and managing mine waste to prevent environmental disasters. Community engagement: Working with local communities to ensure that mining benefits are shared and that local people are protected from negative impacts. Renewable energy: Transitioning to renewable energy sources such as solar, wind, and hydropower to reduce carbon emissions. Land rehabilitation: Restoring mined land to its natural state or to a productive use after mining operations cease. Gold Production Statistics: How Much Gold Do These Mines Produce? To put the scale of these operations into perspective, here is a quick summary of estimated annual gold production from the top mines: Muruntau (Uzbekistan): ~2,000,000 oz/year Carlin Trend (USA): ~3,000,000 oz/year (combined region) Grasberg (Indonesia): ~1,000,000 oz/year Olympic Dam (Australia): ~140,000 oz/year Pueblo Viejo (Dominican Republic): ~800,000 oz/year Lihir (Papua New Guinea): ~750,000 oz/year Kibali (DRC): ~750,000 oz/year Cortez (USA): ~600,000 oz/year Boddington (Australia): ~700,000 oz/year Goldstrike (USA): ~500,000 oz/year The Future of Large-Scale Gold Mining The gold mining industry is rapidly evolving. With many high-grade, easily accessible deposits already mined out, companies are increasingly turning to deeper, more complex ore bodies and remote locations. Technological innovation is playing a critical role, with advances in: Automation and robotics: Autonomous haul trucks, drilling equipment, and processing systems are reducing costs and improving safety. Artificial intelligence: AI and machine learning are being used to optimize ore processing, predict equipment failures, and improve geological modeling. Greener processing: Research into non-cyanide leaching methods and more environmentally friendly processing techniques is gaining momentum. Deep mining technology: Advances in refrigeration, ventilation, and rock mechanics are making it possible to mine at increasingly greater depths. Conclusion The world’s biggest gold mines are extraordinary industrial operations that combine cutting-edge technology, massive infrastructure, and thousands of skilled workers to extract one of humanity’s most precious resources. From the vast open pits of Muruntau and Carlin Trend to the deep underground operations at Grasberg and Kibali, each mine presents unique challenges and solutions. As global demand for gold remains strong — driven by jewelry, electronics, investment, and central bank reserves — these operations will continue to evolve and innovate to meet the needs of the modern world. Whether you are an investor, a mining professional, or simply curious about where your gold comes from, understanding the scale and complexity of these operations provides a fascinating insight into one of the world’s most enduring industries.

items.title: J2 Metals inicia estudio magnético con drones en el proyecto Sierra Plata en Guerrero
items.summary: J2 Metals Inc. anunció la movilización de equipos técnicos para llevar a cabo el primer estudio geofísico magnetométrico aéreo de alta resolución mediante vehículos aéreos no tripulados (UAV) en su proyecto de plata, oro y antimonio Sierra Plata. La propiedad se localiza en el histórico distrito minero de Zacualpan-Taxco, en... The post J2 Metals inicia estudio magnético con drones en el proyecto Sierra Plata en Guerrero appeared first on Mining México .
items.content: J2 Metals Inc. anunció la movilización de equipos técnicos para llevar a cabo el primer estudio geofísico magnetométrico aéreo de alta resolución mediante vehículos aéreos no tripulados (UAV) en su proyecto de plata, oro y antimonio Sierra Plata. La propiedad se localiza en el histórico distrito minero de Zacualpan-Taxco, en el estado de Guerrero, México. El programa de intensidad magnética total (TMI) será ejecutado por la firma mexicana JM Geoconsultores, con sede en Querétaro. La campaña aérea abarcará una superficie aproximada de 1,885 hectáreas y comprenderá un total de 562 kilómetros lineales de vuelo. De acuerdo con las especificaciones técnicas compartidas por la compañía, el diseño del estudio contempla un espaciamiento de 50 metros entre líneas y vuelos programados a una altitud constante de 50 metros sobre el nivel del suelo, utilizando un magnetómetro de alta resolución montado sobre una plataforma de dron DJI Matrice. El objetivo principal de este despliegue tecnológico es recopilar datos geofísicos modernos que permitan construir un modelo estructural tridimensional del yacimiento. Thomas Lamb, director ejecutivo de J2 Metals, destacó que la integración de esta información aérea con los mapeos geológicos terrestres en curso proporcionará una imagen precisa de las estructuras que albergan la mineralización, lo cual resultará indispensable para definir los objetivos prioritarios de una futura campaña de perforación. Te puede interesar: J2 Metals descubre tres minas históricas en su proyecto Sierra Plata en Guerrero Paralelamente, la minera informó que sus actividades de exploración terrestre avanzan bajo la supervisión de su gerente de país en México, Carlos Cham. El equipo técnico continúa realizando muestreos de roca y documentando antiguas obras mineras en el sector occidental de la propiedad, al tiempo que gestiona los permisos ambientales necesarios ante las autoridades correspondientes. Una vez concluida la adquisición de datos del dron, J2 Metals contratará asesoría geofísica independiente para realizar una inversión 3D de los resultados estructurados. Para más información del sector minero y energético también puedes consultar el portal de noticias en tiempo real de Industry News Mx . items.title: UMS and Renault Trucks mark ten years of partnership in Guinea bauxite
items.summary: For the past ten years, UMS United Mining Supply, a specialist in mining, industrial and oil transport and logistics in West Africa, has relied on Renault Trucks to carry out its operations in particularly demanding environments. Renault Trucks and UMS have built a long-term partnership combining vehicles, services, expertise and operational support. Today, 350 Renault The post UMS and Renault Trucks mark ten years of partnership in Guinea bauxite appeared first on International Mining .
items.content: For the past ten years, UMS United Mining Supply, a specialist in mining, industrial and oil transport and logistics in West Africa, has relied on Renault Trucks to carry out its operations in particularly demanding environments. Renault Trucks and UMS have built a long-term partnership combining vehicles, services, expertise and operational support. Today, 350 Renault K Trucks are engaged in the heart of UMS’s mining operations in Guinea. In this country, the world’s leading exporter of bauxite, trucks are constantly evolving to ensure the transport of ore between extraction areas and port infrastructure. In the Boké region, where several major mining operations are concentrated, Renault K Trucks transport bauxite from the extraction areas to the port infrastructure. The trucks operate 24/7 on demanding terrain and under particularly high load loads. Each truck travels more than 190,000 kilometres per year, or more than 66 million cumulative kilometres across the fleet. The 350 Renault K Trucks dedicated to these operations total more than 50 million kilometres per year. Initiated in 2016 with the support of local distributor TGH Plus, the partnership between Renault Trucks and UMS has continued to strengthen. In ten years, 600 Renault Trucks trucks have been delivered to support the company’s mining and logistics operations. In addition to supplying the trucks, Renault Trucks works closely with the UMS teams to continuously improve the performance of the fleet. Telematics data is jointly analysed monthly to optimise fuel consumption and operating cycles. Feedback from the field also helps to develop the vehicles. Some adaptations developed to meet the specific needs of UMS are now integrated directly into the solutions offered by Renault Trucks for the most demanding environments. In mining operations, every vehicle downtime can have a direct impact on production. To guarantee the availability of its fleet, UMS relies on its integrated workshops, where more than 150 mechanics, organised in three shifts, are constantly involved. In order to reduce intervention times, Renault Trucks is working with UMS to size and manage the stocks of spare parts present on site. This organisation helps to maintain a high level of truck availability despite the intensity of mining operations. Skills development is another pillar of the partnership. Every year, Renault Trucks provides training courses for UMS mechanics, drivers and trainers. Twice a year, a specialist trainer visits the operating sites to support the local teams in rational driving and safety. This approach is part of a global approach aimed at continuously improving the safety of operations, in particular through the analysis of vehicle routes and operating conditions. Founded in 2001 by Fadi Wazni, United Mining Supply (UMS) is a Guinean company specialising in integrated logistics, serving the mining, industrial and oil sectors in Guinea and West Africa. The group supports operators with logistics solutions adapted to the most demanding environments with its fleet of more than 500 trucks. ISO 9001 certified, UMS says it “places quality, safety and performance at the heart of its activities. Strongly rooted in the local fabric, it employs nearly 98% Guinean employees and actively contributes to the development of national skills. “Through its expertise and commitment to operational excellence, UMS is a key player in the development of logistics and industrial chains in Guinea.” The post UMS and Renault Trucks mark ten years of partnership in Guinea bauxite appeared first on International Mining .

items.title: Charging Power: Lithium as a Critical Metal for the Supply Chain
items.summary: As nations and international organizations set ambitious climate and sustainability goals, commodity prices have soared and supply chains have entered a state of flux. Geopolitical volatility, trade disputes and resource events such as the war in Ukraine and lockdowns in China magnified the trend, exposing the vulnerabilities of Western energy dependence and pushing critical minerals to the forefront for governments and investors. Countries like Canada and the US have established lists of critic
items.content: As nations and international organizations set ambitious climate and sustainability goals, commodity prices have soared and supply chains have entered a state of flux. Geopolitical volatility, trade disputes and resource events such as the war in Ukraine and lockdowns in China magnified the trend, exposing the vulnerabilities of Western energy dependence and pushing critical minerals to the forefront for governments and investors. Countries like Canada and the US have established lists of critical minerals or materials deemed essential to national security and economic prosperity. However, most of these remain sourced from volatile or monopolized foreign jurisdictions. While the energy transitions of the past relied on fossil fuels, the current shift toward sustainability runs on metals. Among the resources at the center of this paradigm shift is lithium. The supply-demand weight The June 2026 edition of UN Trade and Development’s Global Trade Update highlights that critical minerals are reshaping global trade as demand surges. Lithium, specifically, is expected to see a rise in demand by more than 350 percent by 2040. The metal has slowly earned its spot in the renewable energy transition, largely due to its use in manufacturing. Accounting for 87 percent of the total demand is its application in rechargeable batteries for electronics, electric vehicles (EVs) and grid storage. Analysts note that EVs have emerged as a primary catalyst for this historic demand surge, completely transforming the global automotive landscape. Beyond this demand is the issue of concentration, as supply chains remain highly concentrated. In 2025, Australia, Chile and China together produced over 70 percent of the world’s lithium. This leaves behind giants like Canada and the US in terms of securing upstream supply lines, an irony considering their designation of lithium as a critical mineral. “The challenge for many mineral-rich developing countries is that they keep exporting raw materials while higher-value processing and manufacturing take place elsewhere,” the UN noted. Achieving true domestic independence requires scaling up a localized, closed-loop supply chain. This creates an urgent imperative to back domestic projects that offer high-upside resource potential in premier geographic locations. Why jurisdiction and local infrastructure matter It is important to note that not all mineral deposits are created equal. An exploration project situated in a faraway location without any power, roads or water infrastructure faces prohibitively costly capital expenditures that can make such a project uneconomical despite the size of the resources. Conversely, a project located in a favorable mining jurisdiction with inherent logistics will likely traverse the path from exploration to development a little more easily. This is where the power of infrastructure and jurisdiction comes in. By operating in mining-friendly jurisdictions with proven legal stability, companies can benefit from an efficient regulatory process, the presence of local expertise and heavy traffic corridors. “Governments that spend on infrastructure give themselves the best chance to succeed, while underinvestment restrains inclusive economic prosperity,” an article by private equity firm Webber Wentzel wrote. “Mining is directly impacted by a country's infrastructure and ability to move mine product, key equipment, materials, and goods from source to market.” Exploring the power of Apex Resources’ Lithium Creek Nevada ranked first on the global Investment Attractiveness Index in the Fraser Institute's 2025 Annual Survey of Mining Companies and scored a perfect 100 for Policy Perception Index. Vancouver-based exploration company Apex Resources (TSXV:APX,OTCID:SLMLF) is seeking to benefit from this with its flagship Lithium Creek project in Churchill County, Nevada. [Apex Resources: Lithium Creek] Besides its advantageous location, the project addresses the exact infrastructure and logistical challenges currently plaguing the wider battery metals sector. It operates within a highly strategic 30-mile radius of the 2,500-acre Tesla Gigafactory near Reno, the principal North American battery hub and Tesla’s first-ever gigafactory. This proximity to the gigafactory and other core EV manufacturing infrastructure allows Lithium Creek to further its potential as a standard exploration play into a vital logistics asset. This can mean thousands of miles less or no complex international shipping, insulating the asset from cross-border friction and maritime vulnerabilities. Apex Resources also noted that the Inflation Reduction Act states that 80 percent of critical minerals found in EV batteries will have to be extracted or processed in the United States or countries with which it has free trade agreements, or they will have to have been recycled in North America by the end of 2026—an aspect that opens an opportunity. A potential world-scale lithium brine resource What also makes Lithium Creek a beneficial asset is its immense geological potential as a district-scale exploration target. The project targets large, closed-basin environments suitable for substantial lithium brine accumulations like Albemarle's Silver Peak Mine in Clayton Valley, the oldest active lithium production site in the US. The conceptual model for Lithium Creek holds that groundwater recharge from precipitation filters through lithium-rich volcanic material, collecting in a structurally closed basin where it is enriched and concentrated through natural geothermal processes. Lithium Creek is also a candidate for using clean direct lithium extraction while taking advantage of green energy sources available in the region. This will minimize water usage, reduce production times and provide a minimal carbon footprint, allowing for the delivery of a more sustainable lithium supply for America’s battery and EV market. Apex Resources is systematically moving the project through the permitting pipeline ahead of its initial drilling campaigns. Because the property has never been historically drill-tested for deep lithium brines, it represents a fresh, high-impact growth catalyst. Investor takeaway The macroeconomic forces shaping the lithium sector are clear. The increasing adoption of EVs and the need for renewables to be stored in grid storage are at a fast pace, alongside the geopolitical drive to create self-sufficiency with respect to a country’s resource needs. With this, investors looking to gain from the clean energy revolution may find an answer in assets like Apex Resources’ Lithium Creek. The combination of tremendous exploratory potential with proximity to manufacturing facilities advances the power to potentially supercharge the next wave of energy demand. items.title: First Majestic Offloads San Martin Mine in US$90 Million Deal
items.summary: First Majestic Silver (TSX:AG,NYSE:AG) has agreed to sell its past-producing San Martin silver mine in Mexico for US$90 million in cash, furthering the company’s push to monetize non-core assets held on care and maintenance. The perimeter of the sale includes the Jalisco group of properties, covering 5,245 hectares of mining concessions located 250 kilometers north of Guadalajara. First Majestic suspended active mining operations at the San Martin silver mine and placed the facility under care a
items.content: First Majestic Silver (TSX:AG,NYSE:AG) has agreed to sell its past-producing San Martin silver mine in Mexico for US$90 million in cash, furthering the company’s push to monetize non-core assets held on care and maintenance. The perimeter of the sale includes the Jalisco group of properties, covering 5,245 hectares of mining concessions located 250 kilometers north of Guadalajara. First Majestic suspended active mining operations at the San Martin silver mine and placed the facility under care and maintenance in July 2019 due to security concerns and declining operational efficiencies. The buyer is Flextronics Supply and Service, a private Mexican company that operates under Meridian Capital, an investment firm managing mining and energy developments across Mexico, Venezuela and Uruguay. Under a staggered payment structure, Flextronics will pay US$2.5 million at closing, US$2.5 million within 180 days, five annual US$10 million installments, and a final US$35 million payment on August 31, 2032. Closing is targeted for the fourth quarter of 2026, pending Mexican antitrust clearance. The transaction will also transfer all issued and outstanding shares of Minera El Pilon, S.A. de C.V., the subsidiary controlling the San Martin mine. The San Martin deal mirrors the financial architecture used in the company's recent Del Toro exit , where First Majestic traded legacy holding costs for guaranteed near-term liquidity and deferred payouts. The Del Toro silver mine, also located in Mexico, was placed on care and maintenance by First Majestic in January 2020. At the June 22 closing of the Del Toro sale, First Majestic received US$30 million upfront, split between US$20 million in cash and US$10 million in Sierra Madre Gold and Silver (TSXV:SM,OTCQX:SMDRF) common shares priced at C$1.30 each. The remaining US$30 million in potential consideration is bound to three specific development milestones that would each trigger a US$10 million payment. Combined, the two transactions eliminate significant care and maintenance liabilities from First Majestic’s balance sheet, while potentially securing as much as US$150 million in total value. Don’t forget to follow us @INN_Resource for real-time news updates! Securities Disclosure: I, Giann Liguid, hold no direct investment interest in any company mentioned in this article.

items.title: Defiance Silver adquiere el 100% de la propiedad histórica San Acacio en Zacatecas
items.summary: La compañía de exploración minera Defiance Silver Corp. anunció que ha completado con éxito el pago final de la opción para las concesiones mineras de San Acacio. Esta transacción, ejecutada a través de su subsidiaria mexicana Minera Santa Remy S.A., otorga a la firma una participación del 100% en dicha... The post Defiance Silver adquiere el 100% de la propiedad histórica San Acacio en Zacatecas appeared first on Mining México .
items.content: La compañía de exploración minera Defiance Silver Corp. anunció que ha completado con éxito el pago final de la opción para las concesiones mineras de San Acacio. Esta transacción, ejecutada a través de su subsidiaria mexicana Minera Santa Remy S.A., otorga a la firma una participación del 100% en dicha propiedad, considerada un activo clave dentro de su Proyecto de Plata Zacatecas, ubicado en el emblemático distrito argentífero del estado. La propiedad de San Acacio abarca el histórico sistema de vetas Veta Grande, una estructura rica en plata con siglos de tradición minera. El terreno consolidado comprende aproximadamente el 50% de las explotaciones mineras históricas de este sistema, extendiéndose a lo largo de dos kilómetros. Como parte de una expansión estratégica del alcance del proyecto, Defiance también adquirió dos parcelas de terreno adicionales dentro del área de concesión que suman cerca de 24 hectáreas. Para asegurar el control total del activo, la minera realizó un pago final de 2,300,000 dólares estadounidenses al vendedor local. Bajo los términos del acuerdo original, el vendedor conservará una regalía del 2.5% por Retorno Neto de Fundición (NSR); sin embargo, Defiance se ha reservado el derecho de recompra total de dicha regalía por un monto de 2.500,000 dólares. Te puede interesar: SEMARNAT autoriza a Defiance Silver ampliar la exploración en proyecto San Acacio, en Zacatecas Actualmente, la compañía se encuentra en la fase final de un programa de perforación de 10,000 metros en el Proyecto Zacatecas, diseñado para confirmar la extensión de las estructuras mineralizadas tanto en la superficie como a profundidad y validar el modelo geológico para una próxima Estimación de Recursos Minerales. Chris Wright, presidente y director ejecutivo de Defiance Silver, destacó que este hito transforma el portafolio de la empresa, posicionándola como una exploradora líder y sin restricciones en México con el control absoluto de sus principales proyectos en el país. Para más información del sector minero y energético también puedes consultar el portal de noticias en tiempo real de Industry News Mx . items.title: Glenstar Completes Shear-Wave Seismic Survey at Green Monster Project
items.summary: Glenstar Minerals Inc. announces that, further to the recent news release dated June 25th, the Company has completed the Terēan Shear-Wave Seismic Geophysical Survey at its geologically complex Green Monster […]
items.content: Glenstar Minerals Inc. announces that, further to the recent news release dated June 25 th , the Company has completed the Terēan Shear-Wave Seismic Geophysical Survey at its geologically complex Green Monster Project in Clark County, Nevada. The survey was completed and consisted of four (4) shear-wave velocity structure cross sections that were collected along 2,400 ft long arrays. This survey was designed to image cover thickness, depth to bedrock, key structural and lithologic features (faults and voids), and extended from the surface to a 600 foot cross section depth. This Terēan survey was undertaken in order to gain a more detailed understanding of the intricate and complicated geological structures that appear to be present on the property. The final interpretation of the collected data is being compiled and analysed for the final report. The Terēan survey is a detailed geophysical survey that maps a number of critical factors pertaining to underground faults and structural controls on mineralization; including defining depth to competent rock, quantifying overburden, imaging paleochannels & alteration zones, as well as identifying weathered and fractured zones that could indicate the presence of large faults and their particular orientation. Glenstar Geologist Bob Marvin noted that, “The Terēan survey is a powerful tool that will provide Glenstar with detailed information that will help determine what the next phase of drilling and exploration should be as we continue to advance the project. We do know that there is a definite complexity to the geologic structure, or structures, underlying the property and the results of this survey will allow us to gain a far better understanding of how these structures relate to the ore intercept in drill hole GMST-4.” Numerous polymetallic zones were encountered during the Phase 2 program, which consisted of six (6) holes drilled to depths from 500 feet to 585 feet encompassing ~3,320 feet of total drilling across three (3) drill pads. These holes contained highly fractured rock with abundant clay alteration and brightly coloured orange and red oxide minerals and decalcification. Assay results confirmed that the area of interest is significantly mineralized and geologically complex; however, at this early stage of exploration the grades reported are less than expected while at the same time raising a number of interesting questions. As a result of this geological complexity, further geophysical work is required to address the data that is inconclusive and provide a clearer understanding of the type of structure and system contained within the property. Project Background & Recent Exploration Work The Green Monster Property is comprised of 35 federal lode claims covering ~700 acres located in Clark County, Nevada, on the west trending spur of the Spring Mountains and is approximately 40 miles southwest of Las Vegas. Until the recent drill programs conducted in May of 2025 and February of 2026, no drilling was ever conducted on the property. The Company’s previous identification of robust nickel-copper with anomalous cobalt from sampling work done in 2022 indicated that several targets were ideal for shallow RC drilling (see news releases dated May 28, 2025, and July 16, 2025). Glenstar acquired the Green Monster Property and conducted initial groundwork in 2022 that included reconnaissance geologic mapping, surface rock sampling, soil sampling, and a drone magnetic survey. Channel sampling across the exposed back of a raise off the main shaft returned 1.18 meters of 3.77% Cu (Copper), 3.06% Ni (Nickel), 0.21% Co (Cobalt) and 6.83% Zn (Zinc). These values are well in excess of select dump samples from historical underground workings and represent in-place, vein style mineralization. Sampling of oxide and sulfide bearing boulders directly downhill of the patented workings has confirmed the presence of very high zinc (>10%) and silver (>200ppm), as well as copper, uranium, and lead. (Sampling results provided above were previously published in the Green Monster Project NI 43-101 Technical Report dated June 20, 2023, Section 7.5). About Glenstar Minerals Inc. Glenstar is a mineral exploration company with a focus on polymetallic minerals. These elements are classified as critical minerals and are essential in the manufacturing of sophisticated electronics and other vital energy technologies. The Company’s mission is to leverage its knowledge and connections to explore, acquire, and develop critical mineral and energy metal properties throughout the world. Glenstar’s shares trade on the Canadian Securities Exchange (CSE) under the symbol “GSTR”, on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange under the symbol “VO20”, and on the Over-the-Counter market (OTCQB) in the United States under the symbol “GSTRF”. Robert Marvin, P.Geo (ONT) is the qualified person as defined by National Instrument 43-101 and is the independent consulting geologist for Glenstar Minerals Inc., who has examined the Green Monster and Wildhorse properties on the ground numerous times since 2022 and 2024 respectively. All fieldwork relating to geologic observations and sampling as reported herein, has been directly overseen by Mr. Marvin who supervised the preparation of, and has reviewed and approved, the technical information in this release. ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD “David Ryan” President & CEO Further information regarding the Company can be found on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca, by visiting the Company’s website at www.glenstar.ca or by contacting the Company directly at 604-449-2810. items.title: Weichai’s LOVOL delivers fleet of LT105E pure electric mining trucks
items.summary: The level of demand for new energy mining trucks in China, underpinned by the government’s dual carbon strategy that aims at peak carbon emissions before 2030 and carbon neutrality by 2060, has led a number of new companies to enter the market. Recently, several LOVOL 70 t payload LT105E pure electric wide body mining trucks The post Weichai’s LOVOL delivers fleet of LT105E pure electric mining trucks appeared first on International Mining .
items.content: The level of demand for new energy mining trucks in China, underpinned by the government’s dual carbon strategy that aims at peak carbon emissions before 2030 and carbon neutrality by 2060, has led a number of new companies to enter the market. Recently, several LOVOL 70 t payload LT105E pure electric wide body mining trucks were delivered to a large-scale open-pit mining project in southern Hunan. The company stated: “With its robust new energy equipment capabilities, LOVOL Heavy Industry injects green momentum into earthwork transportation operations in mining areas, helping mines accelerate their upgrade toward efficient, energy-saving, and safe modern green mines.” LOVOL Heavy Industry is controlled by Weichai – which is a global player in high performance engines; but it is diversifying into new energy powertrains as well. In May 2026 it said that its all-electric power systems for mining trucks have accumulated over 1 million hours of operational runtime, mainly in the Ordos region of Inner Mongolia, it says helping customers reduce their operating costs by 66%. Weichai supplies batteries, motors and electronic controls for BEVs. It is also a major in hydraulic systems, as Weichai also owns LHY Powertrain – the former Linde Hydraulics. In addition to the LT105E, LOVOL produces the 90 t class LT130E. It also offers the LT110H and LT130H diesel models plus the LT130i methanol powered range extended autonomous truck. In large rigid trucks it supplies diesel engines that can power 100 ton to 400 ton class trucks and trying to displace Western mining truck engines from Cummins and MTU is a major strategic focus for the company. In April 2026 Weichai announced that a Chinese made 300 ton truck equipped with its 12M55 engine had undergone 18,000 hours of successful continuous practical testing under heavy-load and high-intensity continuous operation conditions in mines operated by coal giant CHN Energy. This is an NTE360 truck built by Inner Mongolia North Hauler Joint Stock Co (NHL) in Baotou. Weichai showed off NTE360 mining trucks from NHL with its 12M55 engine in April, including one unit with over 18,000 hours The mining area LOVOL says has a large annual designed capacity, with high intensity of open-pit earth and rock transport, steep gradients, and frequent daily round trips, all of which place strict requirements on vehicle load capacity, climbing performance, endurance stability, all-weather reliability, and energy-saving and environmental protection attributes. LOVOL: “Traditional fuel vehicles have high operating costs and large exhaust emissions, making it difficult to adapt to current green mine development policies and the operational needs of cost reduction and efficiency improvement. After extensive on-site research and comparisons of product brands, the client ultimately selected the LOVOL LT105E pure electric mining truck as the main transport equipment in the mining area.” The LOVOL LT105E pure electric mining truck it says is specially designed for high-intensity earthwork transfer in open-pit mines. Paired with a 40 m³ reinforced rectangular large-capacity cargo box, it offers “outstanding single-trip carrying capacity, effectively reducing round trips and greatly improving mine area transfer efficiency. Relying on a professional mining chassis design, it easily tackles gravel, steep slopes, muddy and rugged terrain, and its powerful performance perfectly suits the working environment of mountain mining areas.” The vehicle is equipped with LOVOL’s self-developed drive system, featuring a high-power motor paired with Weichai New Energy’s dedicated transmission. It offers “strong uphill performance under heavy loads and quick power response, ensuring stable and reliable operation even during long, high-intensity work. The 425 kWh lithium iron phosphate power battery features an IP68 protection rating, confidently withstanding harsh conditions such as mining dust, rain, and mud; Fast charging technology can complete resupply within one hour, perfectly adapting to the mining area’s multi-shift uninterrupted operation needs and maximising equipment attendance rates.” Leveraging the development opportunities of the dual carbon strategy, LOVOL Heavy Industry adds that it continues to deepen its presence in the new energy equipment sector for mines, “constantly iterating on various types of mining transportation equipment and deeply adapting to diversified heavy-duty conditions such as open-pit mines and aggregate materials. In the future, LOVOL Heavy Industry will continue to focus on technological innovation as its core, leverage its robust product capabilities and a comprehensive service system, and continuously empower the green and low-carbon transformation and upgrading of traditional mining, helping more mining projects achieve win-win economic and ecological benefits.” The post Weichai’s LOVOL delivers fleet of LT105E pure electric mining trucks appeared first on International Mining .

items.title: Terronera impulsa producción de plata de Endeavour Silver en 2T26
items.summary: Endeavour Silver Corp. reportó sus resultados de producción correspondientes al segundo trimestre de 2026, periodo en el que alcanzó la extracción de 1,943,955 onzas de plata y 10,474 onzas de oro. Al combinarse con metales base, la empresa totalizó una producción equivalente de plata de 3.4 millones de onzas. Con... The post Terronera impulsa producción de plata de Endeavour Silver en 2T26 appeared first on Mining México .
items.content: Endeavour Silver Corp. reportó sus resultados de producción correspondientes al segundo trimestre de 2026, periodo en el que alcanzó la extracción de 1,943,955 onzas de plata y 10,474 onzas de oro. Al combinarse con metales base, la empresa totalizó una producción equivalente de plata de 3.4 millones de onzas. Con estas cifras, el acumulado en lo que va del año ascendió a 6.8 millones de onzas equivalentes de plata, consolidando 3,819,329 onzas de plata y 22,215 onzas de oro. Dan Dickson, director ejecutivo de Endeavour Silver, detalló que la compañía avanzó con éxito en sus prioridades operativas globales. El incremento en la producción consolidada de plata, que fue un 31% superior a las 1,483,736 onzas registradas en el segundo trimestre de 2025, se debió en gran medida a la aportación de la mina Terronera, que aportó 608,347 onzas tras haber alcanzado la producción comercial, y a los beneficios derivados de la expansión en la unidad Kolpa. Asimismo, la producción consolidada de oro aumentó un 35% frente al mismo periodo del año anterior, respaldada por 7,666 onzas provenientes de Terronera. Estos resultados compensaron los impactos de la venta de la mina Bolañitos, concretada a inicios de año. A nivel de unidades, Terronera continuó con la optimización de su planta y mantuvo su rendimiento en línea con lo previsto. Por su parte, la mina Kolpa, ubicada en Perú, procesó 233,408 toneladas en el trimestre luego de expandir la capacidad de su planta a 2,500 toneladas por día en marzo pasado, logrando un aumento del 19% en su producción de plata respecto al primer trimestre de 2026. Te puede interesar: Torex Gold reporta sólida producción de oro en 2T26; mantiene metas anuales En contraste, Guanaceví en México reportó estabilidad en su procesamiento y un volumen 4% superior en rendimiento, aunque su producción de plata cayó un 30% debido a la explotación temporal de zonas con menores leyes minerales y una mayor dilución. En el renglón comercial, Endeavour logró colocar 2,086,717 onzas de plata y 10,823 onzas de oro durante el trimestre. Para más información del sector minero y energético también puedes consultar el portal de noticias en tiempo real de Industry News Mx . The post Terronera impulsa producción de plata de Endeavour Silver en 2T26 appeared first on Mining México .
